title: Coptophyllum fulvum
taxonomy: Coptophyllum fulvum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, which is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises over 13,000 species of plants, many of which are economically important for their medicinal or ornamental value. Coptophyllum fulvum is further classified as a member of the genus Coptophyllum, which is a group of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The exact classification of Coptophyllum fulvum may be subject to change as new research and discoveries are made. Coptophyllum fulvum is likely to be classified using the APG IV system, which is a widely accepted system for classifying flowering plants.

intro: Coptophyllum fulvum, also known as "Kadu bilam", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Coptophyllum fulvum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coptophyllum fulvum is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as teas, infusions, and topical creams.
